title: alnus hirsuta var. emarginata
intro: Alnus hirsuta var. emarginata, also known as the Alaskan alder, is a variety of the Alnus hirsuta plant, which is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Alnus hirsuta var. emarginata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
